alert_summary: Single IP spraying authentication failures across many accounts; credential stuffing pattern with 120+ distinct users targeted.

reasons.challenge_id: Aligned with credential_stuffing (multi-account spray from one source).
reasons.severity: Medium: broad user impact but no confirmed account takeover in the aggregated window; escalation if successes appear.
reasons.alert_summary: Highlights single-source spray and scale (distinct_users_targeted) from credential_spray rule context.
reasons.remediation_proposal: Rate-limit and temporarily block the source IP; force password resets for impacted accounts; enable MFA; review lockout and CAPTCHA policies on the auth endpoint.

detection.attack_type: Many distinct users failed auth from one IP — spray pattern, not focused SSH BF.
attacker_ips: Single origin for the majority of multi-user failures in the window.
victim_accounts: Application account with high failure volume vs baseline.
Batch account co-targeted in the same bursts.
detection.attack_start_time: Start when multi-user failure density crosses the rule threshold.
detection.attack_end_time: End of active phase before marginal failure rate.
detection.indicators: rule_ids includes credential_spray; distinct_users_targeted summarizes spray breadth.

exhaustive_analysis: From 09:30 to 09:55 UTC one source (203.0.113.50) generated many auth failures across many accounts (svc_api, batch_user among others). Pattern is credential stuffing/spray: one attacker, wide victim set. distinct_users_targeted quantifies scope. Actions: MFA, temporary block, account review.
remediation_proposal: 1) Block 203.0.113.50 at WAF/API gateway. 2) Invalidate sessions for high-risk accounts; mandatory MFA enrollment. 3) Tune credential_spray thresholds and add progressive delays. 4) Check for successful logins from the same IP in adjacent windows.
